message: Jake
Yuriko
−　A few days later　−
The doubts that sprouted during the party settled within my heart.
I don’t know if the serial killings that happened in New York are the work of the same perpetrator.
But it’s hard not to think that one of the people who gathered at the party might be the culprit behind the New York incidents.
It may seem like a very simple viewpoint, but both Eva and Cindy are at least involved in the gossip articles about Guy.
I can't believe that Kristy would be the culprit, though.
But in terms of motive, Kristy seems the most suspicious.
What am I even thinking, I have no evidence, and even more so, I can't suspect her, who is my friend.
I'm probably just feeling down because Jennifer was all over the director.
Good morning.
Were you maybe asleep?
I wasn't sleeping, I was deep in thought.
Were you pondering how to get more requests in?
Not exactly.
Oh, I’m sorry, please don’t get mad.
Now, let’s take our seats and get to work!
Oh?
He isn’t here.
There was no sign of him in the office.
He might have already gone home, but I think he could at least leave a note or two.
ＲＲＲＲＲＲＲＲＲＲＲＲ
Yes, speaking of which,
Ash
Hello, are you coming by soon? Only if you’re free though.
Oh, I’m free, I’ll be there shortly.
Should I say it was a divine intervention? I decided to head to Rain Tree, inspired by Ash's invitation.
Welcome back, are you off for the day?
Ah, Boss. Thank you for your hard work.
See
Talk
Listen
Thinking
Moving
System
The surroundings
about Meryl
about Kristy
about Tony

All I can see is my desk, the phone, and a potted plant with leaves starting to turn brown,
and the figure of
not bad for an office of a rookie detective with little achievement. There's a sofa for guests, and I can even brew coffee.
an office desk for me and
a rather shabby potted plant and a not-so-old computer.
Thanks to being liberated from paperwork for the past few days and getting to pretend to be a detective, I seem to be in a good mood.
It looks like they're looking down on me with a knowing expression. If you have time to think badly, why don’t you just sort out the invoices?
It’s great to be lively. However,
they tend to work more efficiently when they're a little under the weather.
The party was fun, wasn't it?
Glad you’re at peace.
Oh, I too have come to a certain conviction regarding the serial murder case. Hehe, it was a meaningful party.
Really!?
Hehe
Oh! Speaking of which, that key
In the end, whose was it anyway?
Maybe it was dropped by Meryl, who knows.
Do you think so?
If you give it to Ash, he'll find out where the key is from.
Then it feels like Ash is the detective, doesn’t it? Boss has no pride.
Boss!
Pull yourself together!
But what can you do? Ash is more specialized in that kind of job.
How pathetic.
At least be more aware that you're a detective!
About Meryl,
I was really shocked.
Anyone would be shocked if someone suddenly barges in like that.
But I feel like I understand how she feels.
Gai,
isn't the culprit, so there's that.
Did Meryl go back to the hotel alone after that?
I think she probably did.
I wonder if she walked down 5th Street in that loungewear style. She seemed really drunk, but was she okay?
Meryl, isn’t that a bit bad?
Didn’t you think Kristy was unusually cheerful?
Of course, she would be cheerful, it’s been a while since she had a two-shot with her beloved. She probably forgets how dark everything was.
Well, I guess that's true.
Boss, no jealousy, no jealousy.
But it's concerning because it doesn't mean her mental health issues are completely resolved, but if Gai is around, it'll be fine.
Yeah, as long as Gai is here.
Oh no, Boss, don’t say it like Gai is going to leave again. That’s strange.
Isn’t there no more need to worry about Kristy?
Tony has that criminal face, doesn’t he? It's just a matter of time before he surrenders, right?
Why?
He already whispered perfectly at the party. Hohohoho.
It feels like that, you know.
So, you did something bad to Tony?
'Bad' is something I can't just let slide. I just suggested he should confess, you know.
Doing that, huh?
But that's what my sixth sense is saying.
Oh ho ho ho ho!
Yikes!
I didn't think Guy could look so fine after being held captive.
He must have been made to train hard. Since Nets had him captive, they can't afford to have an unfit player.
But man, Nets sure has a dirty way of doing things.
Guy was all over Kristy. They seemed super lovey-dovey, I was a bit jealous.
Well, isn't she just fine with being hot and heavy with her boyfriend?
Unfortunately, I'm currently looking for a boyfriend.
Guy seems pretty nice, doesn't he?
Did you fight with Jennifer or something?
Not really.
Ah, I knew you were sulking. Why was she so clingy with the director, I wonder?
Oh, maybe she was comforting a lonely director after Meryl left? Yeah, that must be it.
Is that really the case?
I think that's unlikely.
Even the Boss pouts about women issues. Surprising!
Yikes!
The director seems like he's been playing around quite a bit.
Worried, right?
I get that he's shocked by losing Cindy, but recently Meryl hasn't been acting normal.
As I feared, it seems he’s drowned himself in alcohol. If this continues, he'll be a wreck.
That could definitely happen.
I can't just leave him like this.
Since Guy's back, it seems like he has completely bounced back, at least from what I've seen.
Even if it was temporary, Kristy was repeating behavior that was way out of line. It might be too early to feel safe yet.
Should I leave Kristy’s situation to Guy?
I initially suspected Tonny, but I can't see him as the kind of guy who would commit serial murders.
There's no doubt that Tonny was set up by Gordon and forced into illegal activities.
But when it comes to murder, I wonder.
He's always making statements that complicate the situation. I have no intention of taking Tonny's words at face value, but it's sad for those who have been mentioned.
He always seems to be nitpicking others about something. It's no wonder he's disliked.
Neither Guy nor Kristy are completely innocent. It seems Guy is still hiding something, and Kristy...
Looking back, Guy's actions have been quite mysterious as well. Even if he claimed to be under house arrest, he could at least have made a phone call.
No, it might be that he didn’t call because he wanted to sever ties with Kristy.
I wonder when it was that I stopped having long conversations with Guy.
Before I knew it, we had grown distant and I couldn’t even tell what the other was thinking.
Isn't Jennifer getting a bit too close with Director Fabar? Could it be that they have that kind of relationship?
Jennifer is what you’d call...
a woman who sleeps with anyone.
Was that the reason she got involved with me? I wonder.
I should stop thinking pessimistically. I haven't heard anything from Jennifer herself, after all.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
